name: liner
description: Liner elements support a flexible host surface (tunnel lining, sprayed concrete, etc.) with bidirectional coupling springs to the host zones. Property keyword set is binary-validated against FLAC2D 9.0 ('structure liner property ?').

notes: FLAC2D liners use bending/section properties similar to beams. 'thickness' (common to FLAC3D shells) is NOT a FLAC2D liner property — use cross-sectional-area and moi instead.
'thermal-expansion' is NOT a liner property in FLAC2D 9.0.
These keywords drive the coupling on the default (side-1) face.
The '-2' suffixed keywords configure the SECOND side of a double-sided liner.
Activate side-2 coupling per liner with the 'effective' property keyword on the relevant create / property command.

common_patterns.name: FLAC2D liner with single-sided coupling
commands: structure liner property young 2e10 poisson 0.2 cross-sectional-area 0.25 moi 1.3e-3 coupling-stiffness-normal 1e9 coupling-stiffness-shear 1e9 coupling-cohesion-shear 1e5 coupling-friction-shear 30

common_patterns.name: Double-sided liner
commands: structure liner property coupling-stiffness-normal 1e9 coupling-stiffness-shear 1e9 coupling-stiffness-normal-2 1e9 coupling-stiffness-shear-2 1e9

notes: FLAC2D 9.0 does NOT accept 'structure shell', 'structure geogrid', 'structure dowel', or 'structure hybrid' subcommands — these are 3D-only structural element types ('Bad conversion of parameter number 2').
Use FLAC3D documentation for these element-type property keyword sets.

flac3d_liner_property_set.description: FLAC3D liner uses a DIFFERENT property keyword set than FLAC2D liner. FLAC3D models liners as a shell-formulation surface (area-based) while FLAC2D models them as a beam-formulation line (length-based).
notes: The FLAC3D liner property set: density, range, thermal-expansion, thickness, effective, slide, slide-tolerance, and all 12 coupling-* / coupling-*-2 keywords (binary-verified via 'structure liner property ?').
The FLAC2D liner property set (documented in this file's property_groups) shares only the coupling-* and slide-* keywords with FLAC3D.

flac3d_shell_property_set.description: FLAC3D shell property keyword set (binary-verified via 'structure shell property ?'). Shells are FLAC3D-only.
notes: FLAC3D shells take only the basic surface-element properties; mechanical stiffness is set via a separate shell constitutive model.

flac3d_geogrid_property_set.description: FLAC3D geogrid property keyword set (binary-verified via 'structure geogrid property ?'). Geogrids are FLAC3D-only.
notes: Geogrids use SHEAR-only coupling (no normal direction): the bare 'coupling-cohesion / -friction / -stiffness' keywords without the '-shear' suffix.
